summary.intent: Add a 5-second sleep after FBC build job triggers in olm_bundle_konflux.py to prevent timestamp collisions when multiple FBC jobs start simultaneously.
summary.outcome: Identified that only the non-openshift fallback path (when no OCP_TARGET_VERSIONS is defined) needs the sleep added. Created a plan for the change, but the actual git workflow (branch creation, commit, push, PR) was not yet executed.

repo: Repository uses 'origin' for user fork (ashwindasr/art-tools) and 'prod' for upstream (openshift-eng/art-tools), despite CLAUDE.md referencing a non-existent 'dev' remote
Layered operators (non-openshift groups like OADP/MTA/MTC) trigger multiple FBC builds, while openshift groups trigger only one

code.finding: FBC build triggering has three paths: (1) non-openshift groups with OCP_TARGET_VERSIONS trigger multiple builds with 10s sleep already present, (2) non-openshift fallback without OCP_TARGET_VERSIONS needs sleep added, (3) openshift groups trigger single build and don't need sleep
Groups are distinguished by whether they start with 'openshift-' prefix to determine FBC build strategy

workflow: When adding delays to prevent job collisions, only add them where multiple jobs can actually be triggered - avoid unnecessary delays in single-job paths
Timestamp collisions can occur when multiple Jenkins jobs start simultaneously

friction: Initial approach added sleeps to all three start_build_fbc call sites before realizing openshift-group path only triggers one job
Documentation mismatch between CLAUDE.md (references 'dev' remote) and actual git remote configuration
open_items: Revert the unnecessary sleep added after the openshift-group start_build_fbc call
Complete git workflow: create branch, commit changes, push to origin, and open PR against prod/main
